name: Troels Holger Vaaben
tagline: Cancer • Microbiome • Synthetic Biology • Translational Research

paragraphs: I work at the interface of microbiome research, cancer biology, and translational experimental systems, combining bioinformatics with hands-on model work to study host–microbe interactions in disease. My computational work centers on metagenomics, pangenomics, transcriptomics, metabolomics, and multi-omics integration, while my experimental experience includes C. elegans, 3D cell models, patient-derived organoids, high-content imaging, and mouse models of cancer, metabolism, and inflammation.

What I do best is connect these layers in a way that is useful for real projects: shaping study design, making complex datasets interpretable, and keeping the biology, models, and analysis aligned. If you are working on a data-driven microbiome project and want to collaborate across both dry-lab and wet-lab contexts, feel free to get in touch.
